entry.title: Re: TM Transportation Management (Raw Material PO with capitalizable cost + Integration with Materia
entry.content:
Hi Arturo

we implemented TM processes in a customer SAP system S/4H 2023 for transport costs related to sales and transport cost related to internal transfer between plants of same company.

In the sales process we allocate transport costs to COPA market segments related to sales order and it's working properly.

For transport costs related to stock transfer orders the system is not allocating costs to materials, no PRD postings.

Could you please give as some suggestions on how to set-up the cost allocaton? Please consider we are in Italy and we activated the purchase account.

Thanks in advance for your help

Sabrina

entry.title: SAP screen persona - Mobgui (RF via webpage) - run script on specific screen only
entry.content:
Hi,

I am working with SAP Screen Personas in MobGUI (WebGUI) and struggling to run a script only on a specific RF screen.

Standard approaches like:

  • session.info.screenNumber
  • program/screen detection
  • /SCWM/RF_TECH_TITLE

do not work reliably or are not available in WebGUI.

Goal

Simplify RF counting (IVMCOU😞

  • current: scan HU → manual ENTER → F2
  • target: scan HU → auto ENTER → auto F2

(and yes, tried customization with AI but after lot of hours was not succesfull)

Issue

Field-based detection (findById, .visible, .text) partly works, but RF screens are reused, so the script executes on multiple screens.

Also, combining ENTER + F2 in one script causes issues (execution order / “control not found”).

Question

What is the recommended way to reliably identify a specific RF screen/step in Personas with MobGUI?

Is there any better approach than combining multiple field checks (positive + negative)?

entry.title: Re: ABAP Extensibility Guide – Clean Core for SAP S/4HANA Cloud - August 2025 Update
entry.content:
Hi @Apurv ,
No that option would definitely be a Level D, as it will create a technical debt. When it comes to upgrade your system, you will need to cater for it manually. So not to go with that option, if you want to be on clean core.

The idea is to concatenate all fields from the child entities into a separate view and create an association to it in the root entity .”

entry.title: Das Business Transformation Framework und Lab von SAP
entry.content:
In der monatlichen Kaffee-Ecke der SAP Training und Change Management Community stand diesmal ein Thema im Mittelpunkt, das über das klassische Change Management und Training hinausgeht: Business Transformation als ganzheitliche Unternehmensaufgabe. Katja Wenzel und Christina Lehmann, beide im Bereich Strategy und Business Architecture Advisory bei SAP tätig, gaben Einblicke in ein Framework, das SAP entwickelt hat, um Transformationsvorhaben strukturierter und messbarer zu gestalten.

Warum scheitern Transformationen immer wieder an denselben Stellen?

Business Transformation ist kein neues Thema. Und dennoch zeigt die Praxis, dass viele Vorhaben nicht den Mehrwert liefern, den Unternehmen sich davon versprochen haben. Christina Lehmann beschrieb das Muster, das sie in Projekten immer wieder antrifft: Unternehmen starten mit ambitionierten Transformationszielen, verlieren diese aber im Verlauf aus den Augen. Laufzeiten werden zu lang, Verantwortlichkeiten bleiben unklar oder werden hin- und hergeschoben, Risiken werden unterschätzt oder gar nicht erst thematisiert.

Diese Beobachtung war der Ausgangspunkt für die Entwicklung des Business Transformation Frameworks. Der Anspruch dahinter ist nicht, ein weiteres Projektmethoden-Rahmenwerk zu schaffen, sondern Leitplanken zu setzen, an denen sich Unternehmen über die gesamte Dauer einer Transformation orientieren können. 

Was bedeutet eigentlich mit Business Transformation?

Zur Einstimmung wurden die Teilnehmer gebeten, ihre Assoziationen zum Begriff Business Transformation in einer Mentimeter-Wordcloud zu teilen. Das Ergebnis zeigte die ganze Bandbreite: Innovation, Fortschritt und Neugier auf der einen Seite, Unsicherheit, Überlastung, Widerstand und Angst auf der anderen. Auch Begriffe wie Kommunikation, Kultur, Zielbild, Management Support und überlastete Key User tauchten auf.

Katja Wenzel machte auf einen wichtigen Punkt aufmerksam: Der Begriff Transformation wird von drei Personen auf mindestens fünf verschiedene Arten interpretiert. Genau das ist ein zentrales Problem in der Praxis. Wer nicht dasselbe meint, wenn er über Transformation spricht, wird auch keine gemeinsame Richtung finden.

Ihr Bild für Business Transformation ist das der Raupe, die zum Schmetterling wird. Man kann einer Raupe ein Skateboard geben oder einen kleinen Antrieb anhängen, dann ist sie schneller und effizienter. Aber sie bleibt eine Raupe. Erst wenn sie sich grundlegend verändert, entsteht etwas wirklich Neues. Übertragen auf Unternehmen bedeutet das: Eine Systemeinführung oder eine Brownfield-Konversion bringt Effizienzgewinne und Optimierungspotenziale, aber sie verändert nicht das Grundprinzip. Echte Transformation setzt ein klares Zielbild voraus und den Willen, sich daran konsequent auszurichten.

Dabei ist Transformation kein abgeschlossenes Projekt. Sobald ein Unternehmen einen neuen Zustand erreicht hat, beginnt die Welt sich bereits weiter zu verändern. Das Ziel verschiebt sich, die Anforderungen ändern sich, und die Anpassung muss weitergehen.

Erfolgsfaktoren aus der Community

In einer zweiten interaktiven Runde sammelten die Teilnehmer die ihrer Ansicht nach wichtigsten Erfolgsfaktoren für Business Transformation. Die Ergebnisse deckten sich in weiten Teilen mit dem, was Katja und Christina aus ihrer eigenen Projekterfahrung kennen: klare Vision und Zielbild, offene Kommunikation, Zusammenarbeit über Funktionsgrenzen hinweg, Vertrauen, Kulturwandel, Management Support, Governance und messbare Ergebnisse. Mehrfach tauchten auch Themen wie Sinnhaftigkeit, Transparenz und die Fähigkeit auf, Aufmerksamkeit über lange Zeiträume hochzuhalten. Transformation ist ein Marathon, kein Sprint. Und wie bei jedem Marathon braucht es Zwischenetappen, an denen man Erfolge sichtbar machen und feiern kann.

Das Business Transformation Framework: Sieben Bausteine

Das Framework von SAP bündelt die Erfahrungen aus vielen Transformationsprojekten in sieben Themenbereichen, die zusammen einen Orientierungsrahmen für businessgetriebene Transformation bilden.

1. Transformation Target Alignment

Der erste und grundlegende Baustein ist die Klarheit über das Zielbild. Was will das Unternehmen mit der Transformation erreichen? Wo soll es sich strategisch positionieren? Dieses Zielbild muss nicht nur definiert, sondern auch klar kommuniziert und konsequent verankert werden, auch wenn es sich im Verlauf der Transformation anpassen sollte.

2. Transformation Governance

Ohne klare Steuerungsstrukturen verlieren sich Transformationen in Zuständigkeitsfragen. Dieser Baustein adressiert die Etablierung von Governance-Strukturen, Rollen und Verantwortlichkeiten, die sowohl während der Transformation als auch danach tragfähig sind.

3. Transformation Risk Management

Risiken werden in Transformationsprojekten zu häufig auf Projektrisiken reduziert. Der Framework-Ansatz zielt darauf ab, die Risiken der Transformation selbst frühzeitig zu identifizieren und aktiv zu steuern, einschließlich der Frage, welche Risiken entstehen, wenn die Transformation nicht stattfindet.

4. Value and Performance Steering

Transformation liefert nur dann nachweisbaren Mehrwert, wenn vorher definiert wird, woran man ihn messen will. Dieser Baustein unterstützt Unternehmen dabei, individuelle KPIs zu erarbeiten, die sowohl finanzielle als auch qualitative Dimensionen abdecken können: von Kostensenkung über Prozessstandardisierung bis hin zur Verbesserung der Auditfähigkeit. Die KPIs werden nicht vorgegeben, sondern gemeinsam mit dem Unternehmen erarbeitet.

5. Innovation Adoption

Innovationen werden in vielen Projekten auf später verschoben, bis die technischen Grundlagen stehen. Das ist aus Sicht der Referentinnen ein gravierender Fehler. Innovation muss von Beginn an integriert werden, nicht als Ergänzung, sondern als Teil des Transformationsgedankens.

6. Organizational Change Capability

Dieser Baustein geht über klassisches Change Management im Projekt hinaus. Es geht darum, eine organisatorische Veränderungsfähigkeit aufzubauen, also die Grundlagen zu schaffen, damit eine Organisation dauerhaft in der Lage ist, sich zu verändern. Veränderungsfähigkeit als Kompetenz, nicht nur als Projektmaßnahme.

7. Standardization and Simplification

Komplexität, die sich über Jahrzehnte aufgebaut hat, behindert Transformation. Dieser Baustein beschäftigt sich damit, Strukturen, Prozesse und Technologien zu vereinfachen und zu standardisieren, um Handlungsspielräume zurückzugewinnen.

Das Framework ist technologieunabhängig. Tools wie Signavio oder Business AI können unterstützend eingesetzt werden, sind aber keine Voraussetzung. Auch Partner können in das Modell eingebunden werden, da Transformationen typischerweise in einem Ökosystem stattfinden.

Das Business Transformation Lab

Neben dem Framework wurde ein konkretes Workshop-Format vorgestellt: das Business Transformation Lab. Es richtet sich an Unternehmen, die am Anfang einer Transformation stehen oder ihre bestehenden Vorhaben neu ausrichten wollen.

Das Lab ist ein eintägiges Format, das Kunden nach Walldorf oder Zürich einlädt. Es gliedert sich in zwei Teile. Der erste Teil ist ein inspirativer Abschnitt in einem immersive Experience Raum, in dem neue Ansätze, innovative Konzepte und Transformationsimpulse gemeinsam beleuchtet werden. Der zweite Teil ist eine Ideation Session mit Methoden die man aus dem Design Thinking kennt, in der die gewonnenen Impulse konkretisiert werden: Was sind die individuellen Handlungsfelder? Wie sieht eine erste Roadmap aus? Welche Bausteine des Frameworks sind für dieses Unternehmen besonders relevant?

Das Business Transformation Lab ist als kostenfreier initialer Workshop konzipiert pro Kunde. Die weitere Ausarbeitung des Frameworks erfolgt dann individuell und projektabhängig. 

Ergebnisse aus den Breakout-Sessions

In Gruppenarbeiten diskutierten die Teilnehmer einzelne Bausteine des Frameworks. Ein zentrales Ergebnis war, dass viele der sieben Bereiche als gleichwertig wichtig eingeschätzt werden, auch wenn je nach Unternehmen und Reifegrad unterschiedliche Schwerpunkte gesetzt werden müssen. Besonders hervorgehoben wurden Governance, Zielklarheit und die organisatorische Veränderungsfähigkeit als Bereiche, in denen Unternehmen am häufigsten Nachholbedarf haben. 

Fragen und Antworten

Wie wird Transformation messbar gemacht?
KPIs müssen individuell pro Unternehmen definiert werden. Sie können finanzielle Kennzahlen umfassen, aber auch qualitative Größen wie die Reduktion von Prozessvarianten oder die Verbesserung der Auditfähigkeit. Der Ansatz sieht vor, KPIs gemeinsam mit dem Unternehmen zu erarbeiten, nicht vorzugeben.

Was bedeutet ganzheitlich im Kontext des Frameworks?
Ganzheitlich bedeutet das Zusammenspiel von Strategie, Organisation und Technologie. Organisatorische Aspekte werden in der Praxis häufig unterschätzt und zu spät adressiert. SAP wird oft nur mit Technologie assoziiert, der Framework-Ansatz versucht bewusst, dieses Bild zu erweitern.

Können Partner in das Modell eingebunden werden?
Ja. Transformationen finden in einem Ökosystem statt, und das Framework ist darauf ausgelegt, auch Partner einzubinden.

Ist das Framework an bestimmte Tools gebunden?
Nein. Technologien wie Signavio können unterstützend eingesetzt werden, das Framework selbst ist technologieunabhängig.

Wie wird Governance definiert?
Governance umfasst sowohl Steuerungsstrukturen und Prozesse als auch Rollen und Verantwortlichkeiten. Sie ist sowohl während der Transformation als auch danach relevant.

Wie priorisiert man die sieben Bausteine?
Die Empfehlung ist, den Reifegrad des Unternehmens als Ausgangspunkt zu nehmen und dort anzusetzen, wo die größten Lücken bestehen. Es gibt keine universelle Reihenfolge.

Ist das Business Transformation Lab kostenpflichtig?
Das Lab ist als kostenfreier initialer Workshop konzipiert. Die weitere Ausarbeitung erfolgt individuell im Rahmen eines Projekts.

O-Töne aus der Session

  1. "Man kann einer Raupe ein Skateboard geben, aber sie bleibt eine Raupe." Einzelne Optimierungen sind kein Ersatz für echte Transformation.

  2. "Transformation ist ein Marathon, kein Sprint." Zwischenerfolge sichtbar zu machen ist entscheidend, um die Aufmerksamkeit und Energie über lange Zeiträume aufrechtzuerhalten.

  3. "Wenn man drei Leute fragt, was Business Transformation bedeutet, bekommt man fünf Antworten." Ein gemeinsames Verständnis ist die Voraussetzung für eine gemeinsame Richtung.

  4. "Wer vorher nicht misst, sieht nie, was besser geworden ist." Value and Performance Steering ist kein Nice-to-have, sondern eine Grundvoraussetzung für nachweisbaren Transformationserfolg.

  5. "Veränderungsfähigkeit ist eine organisatorische Kompetenz, keine Projektmaßnahme." Organisationen müssen die Fähigkeit zur Veränderung dauerhaft aufbauen und verankern.

  6. "Innovation darf nicht auf später verschoben werden." Wer Innovationen erst nach der technischen Implementierung einbindet, hat den Kern von Transformation verfehlt.

Praktische Tipps für die Praxis

Zielbild vor Projektstart definieren. Bevor ein Transformationsvorhaben startet, sollte das strategische Zielbild klar formuliert und kommuniziert sein. Ohne gemeinsames Verständnis, wohin die Reise geht, verlieren sich Teams in operativen Details.

KPIs gemeinsam erarbeiten. Messbarkeit ist kein bürokratischer Akt, sondern die Grundlage für Steuerung und Nachweise. Finanzielle und qualitative KPIs sollten früh definiert und regelmäßig überprüft werden.

Governance von Anfang an aufbauen. Verantwortlichkeiten und Steuerungsstrukturen müssen vor Beginn der Transformation etabliert sein, nicht erst wenn Probleme auftreten.

Transformationsrisiken separat betrachten. Projektrisiken und Transformationsrisiken sind nicht dasselbe. Wer nur Projektrisiken managt, unterschätzt die eigentliche Gefahr.

Innovation früh einbinden. Innovative Konzepte und neue Geschäftsmodelle gehören in die Startphase einer Transformation, nicht in eine zweite Phase, die oft nie kommt.

Veränderungsfähigkeit als Kompetenz entwickeln. Change Management im Projekt reicht nicht. Organisationen brauchen eine dauerhaft verankerte Fähigkeit, sich anzupassen und weiterzuentwickeln.

Komplexität aktiv reduzieren. Gewachsene Strukturen und Prozesse regelmäßig hinterfragen und vereinfachen, um Handlungsspielraum für echte Transformation zu schaffen.

Zwischenerfolge sichtbar machen. Lange Transformationen brauchen Etappenziele, die gefeiert werden können, um Motivation und Rückhalt aufrechtzuerhalten.

Resümee

Die Session zeigte, dass Business Transformation mehr ist als eine Systemeinführung oder ein IT-Projekt. Das Business Transformation Framework von SAP bietet einen strukturierten Ansatz in sieben Bausteinen, der Unternehmen helfen soll, Transformation ganzheitlich zu denken und zu steuern: von der Zielklarheit über Governance und Risikomanagement bis hin zu Messbarkeit, Innovation, Veränderungsfähigkeit und Komplexitätsreduktion.

Besonders wichtig ist der Impuls, dass Transformation nicht als einmaliges Projekt verstanden werden sollte, sondern als kontinuierlicher Prozess. Die Raupe, die zum Schmetterling wird, bleibt nicht stehen. Sie entwickelt sich weiter, solange sich die Rahmenbedingungen verändern.

Das Business Transformation Lab bietet einen niedrigschwelligen Einstieg, um gemeinsam mit SAP ein Verständnis für die eigene Transformation zu entwickeln und erste Handlungsfelder zu definieren. Der Ansatz ist technologieunabhängig und auf den jeweiligen Unternehmenskontext zugeschnitten.

Für die Community aus Change Management, Training und Adoption ist diese Perspektive eine wichtige Erweiterung: Veränderungsfähigkeit beginnt nicht im Projekt, sondern muss als organisatorische Grundkompetenz aufgebaut werden. 

Relevante Links und Ressourcen

SAP Business Transformation Framework Labs und weitere Labs 

Podcast zum Business Transformation Framework auf Apple & Spotify  

SAP Learning und Adoption Forum am 11.06ten

entry.title: ABAP Code Reviewer
entry.content:
A new feature was developed based on the insights from the Abap Versions Explorer.

This is a Code Reviewer service which can help easily and fast review all the changes, accept or decline it with comments or even ask AI to create Summary or make technical documentation and so on.

Video demo of the process.

Video demo of the process with Ask AI feature.

By default used Code Reviewer feature. 

To use the Anthropic API, we need to set up an SM59 connection to api.anthropic.com. It is also possible to use the OpenAI API. I will publish the solution if anyone needs it.

The current solution can perform code reviews and generate both technical and functional documentation. By chaining multiple requests together, we can map out the entire project lifecycle description.

entry.title: Email Processing & SOST Behavior in S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I hope you are doing well.

I need your help regarding SOST (Tcode) in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and how the email process is managed.

I am able to maintain email and PDF templates, and for some scenarios (such as Correspondence), I can preview the email. However, this is not the case for all outputs. For example, for payment advices, I can see the PDF generated in the Manage Automatic Payments app (via the “Display PDF” functionality), but I do not see how the email is handled.

I have already maintained my email in the Business Partner as well as in Output Determination, but no email is being sent. I also checked the email monitoring applications, but they only display a status indicator with very limited details.

Could you please guide me on how to properly test and troubleshoot custom email outputs in this context?
If you have any relevant documentation or resources on this topic, I would greatly appreciate it, as I have not been able to find clear information so far.

Thank you very much for your support!

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, 3-system landscape, parallel line

entry.title: Guia para acessar o suporte de SAP Concur
entry.content:
Neste blog você terá apresenta uma visão geral das opções de suporte disponíveis no de SAP Concur.  

Usuários Finais 

Se você é um usuário do SAP Concur e precisa de suporte, existem algumas opções disponíveis:  

User Support Desk (USD):  
Este é um serviço pago do SAP Concur que muitas empresas contratam para receber suporte técnico de primeiro nível. Você pode entrar em contato com o User Support Desk por telefone, chat ou abrindo um ticket de ajuda. As opções de contato podem ser encontradas no menu “Help”, acessado pelo ícone “?” no canto superior direito da página inicial do SAP Concur. Caso a opção “Contact Support” não apareça no menu de ajuda, isso indica que sua empresa não possui o serviço User Support Desk.   

  1. Recursos Internos:  
    São recursos disponibilizados pela sua empresa, normalmente na forma de um sistema interno de tickets, um número de telefone dedicado ou um endereço de e-mail específico para suporte. Muitas empresas utilizam a seção “Company Notes” na página inicial do SAP Concur para divulgar essas opções de ajuda internas.  
    Caso sua empresa não tenha disponibilizado informações de contato para suporte, recomendamos entrar em contato com seu gestor direto. Se ele não puder ajudar, tente falar com o administrador interno do SAP Concur da sua empresa, que geralmente faz parte das equipes de Financeiro, Contabilidade, Folha de Pagamento, RH ou TI.  
  2. SAP Concur Community – Suporte e FAQs:  
    Na comunidade SAP Concur, a seção de Suporte e FAQs reúne soluções para os problemas mais comuns enfrentados pelos usuários do sistema. Há um campo de busca onde você pode digitar palavras-chave ou frases para encontrar rapidamente respostas para sua dúvida.  
  3. Fóruns da Comunidade SAP Concur:  
    A comunidade SAP Concur possui fóruns de produtos onde os usuários podem publicar perguntas e receber respostas de outros membros da comunidade ou de colaboradores do SAP Concur. Você também pode pesquisar para verificar se sua dúvida já foi respondida anteriormente. Caso não encontre o que procura, pode criar uma publicação com sua pergunta.  
    Observação: é necessário ser um membro registrado e estar logado na comunidade SAP Concur para publicar nos fóruns.  
  4. SAP Help Portal:  
    Pode ser acessado diretamente dentro do SAP Concur pelo menu “Help”. Para acessá-lo, clique no ícone “?” no canto superior direito da tela. Você verá uma lista de opções de ajuda com base nos produtos utilizados pela sua empresa.   

Se o seu problema estiver relacionado ao acesso ao SAP Concur, consulte os artigos abaixo:  

  • How Do I Sign In to My SAP Concur Profile? - SAP Concur Community 
  • Why Can't I Sign In to the SAP Concur Tool? - SAP Concur Community 

Administradores  

Se você é um Authorized Support Contact (ASC) da sua empresa ou um administrador responsável por realizar alterações no sistema SAP Concur, faça login no seu perfil SAP Concur, clique no ícone “?” no canto superior direito e selecione “Contact Support” no menu de ajuda.  

Isso permitirá que você:  

  • converse via chat com um representante;   
  • abra um chamado;   
  • ou ligue para o suporte por telefone.   

O número de suporte deve ser utilizado apenas para problemas críticos de prioridade P1 (indisponibilidade do sistema).  

Um problema P1 ou crítico é definido como qualquer situação que cause impactos graves na execução de processos essenciais de negócio ou de TI, sem solução alternativa disponível. Esse tipo de incidente exige atenção imediata porque afeta todos os usuários.  

Exemplos de problemas P1 incluem, mas não se limitam à impossibilidade de:  

  • reservar viagens;   
  • criar relatórios de despesas;   
  • enviar solicitações de viagem;   
  • enviar solicitações de fatura;   
  • acessar o SAP Concur.   

Caso você tenha um problema P1, pode ligar para +1 855-895-4815 e selecionar a opção “Authorized Support Contact” no menu.  

Recursos adicionais para Authorized Support Contacts (ASC)  

O papel de Authorized Support Contact é uma permissão atribuída a determinadas pessoas dentro da organização. Recomenda-se que não haja mais de cinco pessoas designadas como ASC.  

Como ASC, você possui acesso ampliado ao suporte e aos recursos do SAP Concur, incluindo:  

  • suporte técnico através do SAP Concur Support Portal;   
  • atendimento telefônico pelo número +1 855-895-4815 (somente para problemas P1);   
  • esclarecimento de dúvidas sobre faturamento através do Online Billing Portal Guide.  

Obrigada pela leitura!

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Release 2605
entry.content:

entry.title: SuccessFactors 360 Review: How to Ensure Anonymity for All Participants Except for a Specific Role
entry.content:
Background:

360 Review form is tailored to extract 4 new custom competency categories, with each category containing either 5 or 6 unique competencies that are expected to be rated.

The Route Map of the form includes three phases: TD Selects Raters > 360 Evaluation Stage > Completion Stage.

The Originator should be the sole individual able to view all participants by name, while the employee or any other participant should not have access to see any names.

The SAP KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/2068817 explicitly states that the Originator (O) is not supported in this anonymity permission.

The Implementing and Managing 360 Reviews documentation indicates that either EA / EMMM can be utilized as a custom role.

Question:

1.) Can we establish a "Custom" Role (beyond the existing hierarchy of E > EM/EP/EH/EA or an Originator) that can function as an individual capable of initiating a 360 Review for an employee.

2.) What prerequisite actions must be taken to ensure this custom role can be incorporated.

3.) Is it possible for this role to have the ability to view all participants by name while maintaining anonymity for all other participants.

Thanks,
Surya

I created a custom field to display the Net Price in the approval task workflow for Sales Orders in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

The custom field is successfully created and visible in the approval task.
However, I am facing an issue with the calculation logic.

Currently, I am calculating the price by dividing:
Total Value / Quantity

But this does not always return the exact same value as the standard “Net Price” displayed in the Sales Order item, because the standard Net Price is derived dynamically from pricing conditions and pricing procedures.

My requirement is to retrieve the exact same Net Price value that appears in the Sales Order item overview, instead of recalculating it manually.

Could anyone please advise:

  • Is there a released field/API available in Public Cloud for this value?

entry.title: Re: Order confirmation CDS View
entry.content:
Hello @lekha_behera ,

As Order Confirmation form template uses the data source FDP_V1_ORDER_CONFIRM_SRV, you can create a data source extension to enable additional standard fields for usage in your template.
When extending the data source FDP_V1_ORDER_CONFIRM_SRV, tax related fields, e.g.: Tax Class.3 Customer are available for addition.

  • Refer to How to Extend a Custom Form Template with Standard Fields | SAP Help Portal

If the standard fields you require are not available for extension, you can create a custom field and map the value through CDS View access using a custom logic.

  • Refer to How to Extend a Custom Form Template with Custom Fields | SAP Help Portal

Best regards,
Luiza

entry.title: Re: Running KILL

This is what happened:

1. I ran my C++ code with gdb and hit a problem inside transaction.

2. I interrupted that session without COMMIT/ROLLBACK, fixed the issue and restarted the gdb session.

3. At this point the program got stuck in side BEGIN TRANSACTION.

entry.title: SAP Developer News, May 28th, 2026
entry.content:
Items

CodeJam report live from Mexico with Tom and Rich
- SAP CodeJam topics https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam-blog-posts/sap-codejam-topics/ba-p/221407

Building an LLM Agent using CAP

Sapphire 2026 highlights for SAP Business Data Cloud

- “Mastering SAP Data Architecture” new course https://learning.sap.com/courses/mastering-sap-data-architecture

Developer challenges May into June
- Challenge overview (May) https://community.sap.com/t5/integration-blog-posts/may-2026-developer-challenge-from-events-to-intelligence-building-an-event/ba-p/14386772
- Challenge overview (June) https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-cap-blog-posts/june-developer-challenge-cap-knowledge/ba-p/14405157 

Did You Know? #18 – Sharing and synchronising profiles
- ABAP Cleaner https://github.com/SAP/abap-cleaner

Chapter titles

00:00 Intro
00:07 CodeJam report live from Mexico with Tom and Rich
02:00 Building an LLM Agent using CAP
03:20 Sapphire 2026 highlights for SAP Business Data Cloud
05:21 Developer Challenges May into June
07:00 DYK #18 Sharing and synchronising profiles

Transcript

[Tom & Rich]
Hey, everybody. We're coming to you from a Code Jam Roadshow in Mexico. We started the week in Mexico City, Mexico. Rich, you had the first day. What were you teaching? Yeah, so we did ABAP Cloud and Joule, and we had a great day. We went through the exercises and saw how Joule can assist us in creating RAP applications quickly and easily. and everybody seemed to have a good time yeah and then I led day two which was the code-based agents code jam where we were looking at creating agents from scratch also how agents help us with coding activities we gave a little look at the new Joule Studio but also solved a crime who stole our painting from the gallery using multiple agents working together. So we get to see how different personas can be applied to agents, how different levels of knowledge and expertise can be applied to agents and how then they work together in a crew to solve a problem. Fun stuff. Yeah. And here we are Wednesday. It was a travel day to Monterey. I think that's where we are. That's all right. You see the beautiful mountains behind us. outside the city. And the moon, right? Yeah, and a crane, evidently. But now we're going to do it again. So all over again tomorrow, we're doing another code jam here in Monterey. Is it at the SAP offices? Tomorrow's at the SAP offices. Again, we're doing all ABAP cloud and Joule. Some of our friends are coming from Mexico City as well. And yeah, so we'll have a good time again. All right. Yeah. So until next time, maybe we'll be coming to a city near you in the near future check out all the new stuff that we have yep bye for now.

[Ajay]
Hello everyone agentic systems have been gaining significant traction lately and justifiably so they enable the development of ai systems capable of iteratively reasoning and executing toward the goal-oriented solutions while numerous frameworks and libraries exist for building standalone agents The SAP Cloud Application Programming Model now presents a distinct opportunity to architect agents that encapsulate domain -specific knowledge and seamlessly integrate with SAP ecosystem, including its native services and backend systems. We have a tutorial blog post by Johannes Vogt from CAP Enablement Team, where he walks us through the implementation of a lightweight LLM -powered agent leveraging the CAP framework. CAP integrates nicely with identity workflow and allows us to easily expose our services as tools for the agent, while also ensuring that all the necessary service level logic and permissions are applied. Since production-like qualities like authentication, authorization, and database transactions are handled by CAP, we can focus on the domain. There are further links to the blog post that you can explore for advanced topic and use cases. So give it a try today and begin your agentic journey using SAP Cloud Application Programming Model. Links to the blog post will be in the description below.

[Witalij]
If you are interested in all things data but missed Sapphire, or you just want the short version without sitting through hours and hours of sessions, our product expert at SAP, KP Sauer, has you covered. He recorded a few-minute recap of top new features in SAP Business Data Cloud to help you quickly catch up on the most important announcements. Here are some of the key topics he covered in his video. The announcement of the SAP Business AI Platform and what actually changed from an architectural perspective. Why SAP HANA Cloud is becoming a core component of the Business Data Cloud strategy. How Reltio and SAP Master Data Governance fit together inside BDC. And finally, Joule agents operating directly within the business data fabric. So, if you want the high -level takeaways without the noise, this is definitely You'll find the link in the description. Plus, this week, the Mastering SAP Data Architecture course officially went live on learning.sap.com, alongside the launch of the new SAP Certified Data Architect Certification. This is not a generic introduction
course. It is designed for data practitioners and data architects who need to operate on architectural depth and understand how enterprise data strategy is evolving in the AI era. The course covers topics like architecting data strategy for the agentic AI era, applying established frameworks including TOGAF, DAMA DMBOK and the SAP enterprise architecture framework in real-world scenarios and exploring concepts like data mesh, semantic layers, and knowledge graphs for enterprise AI. So if you are working in the enterprise data architecture, analytics, governance, or AI strategy, this is definitely worth your time as well.

[DJ]
The developer challenge for the month of May is coming to an end this week. Antonio did a great job accompanying and guiding us on a journey to learn about event driven architectures and AI. In week one, we acquainted ourselves with events. In week two, we connected to the broker and started to consume those events. In week three, we looked at vectorizing the event payload. And in this final week, week four, we're looking at storing vectors and enabling retrieval augmented generation. That's RAG. While this month's challenge is still active, June is just around the corner. So we wanted to give you a heads up that the next challenge is ready and will start on Monday. June's challenge encourages you to dig into the wonders and delights of the CAP documentation, aka CAPIRE, to unearth truth and beauty in this exemplary and valuable resource. Why? Because today, more than ever, we as developers need to ensure we retain knowledge and double down on our understanding of architectures, toolkits, frameworks, and perhaps most of all, of best practices. More on that in the challenge intro post, which is published now. When the challenge starts, you can also head back to this challenge intro post to find your way to the tasks. Good luck, have fun, and as always, happy learning.

[Sheena]
Did you know that you can maintain a synchronized ABAP profile across your team by creating and sharing a team profile in ABAP Cleaner? Usually, ABAP Cleaner profiles are created locally based on the required rules. Let us consider a scenario where a project team would like to come up with a synchronized profile that can be used by everyone in the team. Here, the lead developer or architect can formulate a profile where all the required rules are activated and this profile is then exported using the export button to a synchronized folder, which may be the team's OneDrive or SharePoint. Team members can then use this profile by clicking on the folders button to add the synchronized folder as an additional read-only source of team profiles. In case of any further changes, we can simply export it again to override the synchronized folder and thereby sharing the updates to everyone, provided it's not the default profile.

entry.title: Re: Not able to add a new Routine Number inside the Prici...
entry.content:
Good day @Ramkinker 

Thank you for your question.

Based on your screenshot and the requirement, the key point is that the old on-premise approach of copying a standard VOFM/R program into a Z routine is not available in the cloud system. In the Public Cloud, pricing routine extensions are only possible through the released pricing BAdIs in the Custom Fields app, and only for the supported pricing use cases such as condition amount, condition base value and pricing requirements. See KBA 2885513 and 2756560 for more insights.

In your case, the BAdIs you already checked are the correct released ones, but they do not provide a changing parameter to replace or overwrite the material group used by the standard routine. This means you cannot create a new routine number in the same way you did in on-premise and use it to change the default material group logic inside the standard pricing routine.

So, in simple terms the following apply:

  • You cannot copy a standard pricing routine into a Z routine like in on premise.
  • You cannot directly change the standard material group determination used by SAP standard pricing routine logic.
  • The pricing BAdIs listed can influence condition amount, condition base value, or whether a condition should be considered but they do not allow you to change the standard material group field used internally by the pricing routine.
  • If your requirement is only to include or exclude a condition based on another material group related value, then the Public Cloud offers you the possible approach of using pricing requirement BAdI.
  • If the requirement is to determine prices or discounts using another material grouping field, the better design is to model this through pricing condition tables, access sequences, custom fields for pricing, or condition records instead of trying to change the standard routine.

There is also extensibility possibility for pricing condition records using custom fields. This means, if the field you need is not the standard material group but a different product attribute, you can evaluate whether it can be added as a custom field for pricing and then used in condition records.

Basically, this means you have two realistic options:

Option 1 (cleanest approach) is for you to use pricing configuration instead of a new routine. If the requirement is to price based on another grouping logic, create or adjust the pricing configuration using:

  • Condition table
  • Access sequence
  • Condition type
  • Pricing procedure
  • Condition records

Option 2 if for you to use a pricing requirement BAdI only to control whether the condition is active. If the requirement is not to change the material group itself but only to decide whether the pricing condition should apply or not, then use:

  • PRICING_DOCUMENT_REQUIREMENT
  • PRCG_DOCUMENT_ITEM_REQUIREMENT

These can be used to return whether a pricing condition should be considered. However, they should not be expected to change the standard material group or the standard access field used for pricing. Note that the logic is used to determine whether a condition is considered during pricing.

For your exact requirement, if you need a new routine number that changes the standard routine’s material group logic, then this is not supported in the cloud system with the released extensibility options.

The best direction is therefore:

  1. Check whether the “other Material Group” can be represented as a standard pricing field, product hierarchy, product attribute or a custom field enabled for pricing.
  2. If yes, create a custom condition table and access sequence using that field.
  3. Assign the access sequence to the relevant condition type.
  4. Maintain the condition records in Manage Price-Sales.
  5. Test the pricing result in a sales order
  6. If the field cannot be made available for pricing and cannot be handled through the released BAdIs, you may consider raising an influence request.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Embracing the Future at Sapphire 2026 - A Journey with Migration and Modernization Assistants
entry.content:
Welcome to Sapphire 2026, where the future meets enterprise transformation! This year, attendees had the privilege of diving into the world of Migration and Modernization Assistants, a vision brought to life by industry leaders Soeren Ruder and Sonja Liénard during a riveting session at the conference.

Reflecting on the Journey

The session kicked off with Soeren, CPO at Cloud ALM, drawing us into the transformational journey that began back in 2021 with the launch of RISE with SAP. Although initially branded as "Business Transformation as a Service," Soeren was candid about the lack of substantial transformation at the onset. However, fast forward to today, and we see a company that has made significant strides with impressive acquisitions like Signavio, LeanIX, and WalkMe, reiterating their commitment to authentic business transformation.

Recognizing that transformation is not without its complexities and costs, Soeren emphasized the role of the latest AI capabilities and emerging technologies in addressing these challenges, marking 2026 as a significant milestone in embedding migration and modernization into the core of business transformation motions.

Introducing the Magnificent Seven: Migration and Modernization Assistants

Soeren's presentation took us through the innovative toolchain designed to minimize risks and costs associated with migration projects. Here’s a glimpse into these groundbreaking assistants:

  1. System Analysis Assistant: The cornerstone of transformation projects, this tool provides insights into system data, custom code, and overall requirements, laying the groundwork for seamless migration.
  2. Data Management Assistant: Tackling data inconsistencies and offering actionable solutions to expedite the migration process, this assistant leverages AI for rapid and effective data cleanup.
  3. Custom Code Assistant: With Joule for Developer, this assistant not only ensures code compatibility with SAP S/4HANA but also dives deep into semantic understanding, offering a path from the complexity of custom code to optimized standard processes.
  4. Configuration Assistant: Automating system configuration by smartly combining best practice models with user-defined scopes, this assistant sets up systems efficiently.
  5. Test Management Assistant: Redefining test case creation with AI, this assistant collaborates with Tricentis to reduce manual effort, accelerating testing phases with precision and automation.
  6. Rollout Assistant: Simplifying regional expansions, this tool enables parallel rollouts by ensuring template compatibility and adaptations.
  7. Project Management Assistant: Moving beyond traditional project oversight methods, this tool makes project management dynamic and responsive, integrating intelligent feedback with a single source of truth for progress tracking.

Live Demonstration: A Real-Time Experience

The session transitioned to an enthralling demo led by Sonja, where attendees witnessed the synergy of these assistants within a unified platform. The demo illustrated processes across three different roles - project manager, custom code lead and developer - demonstrating how these assistants streamline tasks and optimize project execution.

Through strategic task delegation and AI-powered automated code remediation, the demo revealed a future where ERP migration is transformed from labor-intensive endeavors to efficient, outcome-driven processes.

Commitment to Excellence: SAP’s Vision Realized

The announcement of Siemens as an early adopter resonates with SAP’s confidence in these Migration and Modernization Assistants. Siemens’ alignment with SAP's goals of reducing migration efforts is a testament to the transformative power these tools hold in real-world applications.

Explore More at Sapphire 2026

With numerous sessions exploring the depths of data management, custom code optimization, and task automation, Sapphire 2026 promises a wealth of knowledge and innovation. Attendees are encouraged to visit the Agent Led Tool Chain booth for deeper insights and discussions with experts.

As we conclude this session, it's evident that the Migration and Modernization Assistants are not merely tools—they are harbingers of a new era in enterprise transformation. Enjoy the Sapphire experience, and here's to shaping the future of digital business transformation!

Thank you for joining us at Sapphire 2026.

entry.title: Re: find sql anywhere development files
entry.content:
The point is that you seem to be using some sort of make tool. The environment variable is SQLANY17 (don't know where you got the braces from). Many years ago, the make tool we used would have expected $(SQLANY17). I am suggesting to you that you use the internet to find out how to reference an environment variable using whatever make tool you employ. Here is a piece of an ancient makefile that we used 30 years ago.

!ifndef H_DIR                                          
!   define H_DIR $(DBDIR)\h                            
!endif                                                 
!ifndef WSQLH_DIR                                      
!   define WSQLH_DIR $(H_DIR)\wsqlh                    
!endif                                                 
!ifndef NEWDBDIR                                       
!   define NEWDBDIR $(DBDIR)\newstore                  
!endif                                                 
!ifndef HVER_DIR_ROOT                                  
!   define HVER_DIR_ROOT    h\h$(WSQLVER)              
!endif                                                 
!define HVER_DIR            $(DBDIR)\$(HVER_DIR_ROOT)

entry.title: Ledger not active in Chart of Depreciation & FYV mismatch (Copied Chart of Dep. on Practice Server)
entry.content:
hello SAP Experts,

I am currently setting up a configuration on a practice server and am hitting a roadblock while trying to assign/register a new Company Code (GM01) to a Chart of Depreciation.

For context, I copied the Chart of Depreciation (GM01) from another country's standard template (such as US / India) into my current setup. When saving, I am receiving the following two hard errors:

  1. "Fiscal year variants in company code GM01 have differing start dates"

  2. "Ledger Q1 defined in CoCd GM01 is not active in chart of depreciation GM01"

What I have tried so far:

  • I tried creating a new ledger and activating it with the Company Code again, but the system continues to state that the specific ledger is not active.

Since this is a practice environment and the Chart of Depreciation was copied across different country standards, I suspect there is a fundamental mismatch in the underlying calendar/fiscal settings and ledger mappings.

entry.title: Combined Picking in SAP EWM for Multiple Deliveries with Common Products
entry.content:
Combined Picking in SAP EWM for Multiple Deliveries with Common Products

Introduction

In high-volume warehouse environments, it is common to receive multiple outbound deliveries containing identical products for different customers.
Executing picking separately for each outbound delivery often results in:

  • excessive warehouse task creation,

  • repeated travel paths,

  • unnecessary picker movement,

  • increased labor effort,

  • and reduced warehouse efficiency.

SAP EWM provides the capability to optimize this process using Combined Picking, where products from multiple outbound deliveries are consolidated into a single warehouse order for execution.

This article demonstrates a practical warehouse execution scenario involving:

  • 3 outbound deliveries,

  • common products across all deliveries,

  • single combined warehouse order creation,

  • consolidated warehouse task execution,

  • and final distribution back to respective deliveries.

Business Scenario

A warehouse receives 3 outbound deliveries for different customers:

Customer Outbound Delivery

Walmart SuperCenter80010771
Costco Wholesale80010772
Albertsons80010773

All 3 deliveries contain the same products:

  • Product 1

  • Product 2

  • Product 3

Instead of generating separate picking tasks for each delivery, SAP EWM combines the picking quantities into a single warehouse execution process.

Delivery Overview

Delivery 1 – Walmart SuperCenter

Material Description Delivery Qty

250054Product 11 EA
250055Product 22 EA
250056Product 33 EA

Delivery 2 – Costco Wholesale

Material Description Delivery Qty

250054Product 14 EA
250055Product 25 EA
250056Product 36 EA

Delivery 3 – Albertsons

Material Description Delivery Qty

250054Product 17 EA
250055Product 28 EA
250056Product 39 EA

Combined Warehouse Order Creation

SAP EWM combines all 9 delivery items into a single warehouse order in queue:

EACHESPICK

Instead of:

  • 9 individual warehouse tasks,

  • multiple travel paths,

  • and repetitive picking,

the system intelligently consolidates quantities for common products.

This significantly improves:

  • picker productivity,

  • travel optimization,

  • warehouse throughput,

  • and labor efficiency.

Combined Warehouse Tasks

The warehouse picker now executes combined picking quantities:

Material Description Total Qty to Pick UoM

250054Product 112EA
250055Product 215EA
250056Product 318EA

Total:

45 EA across 3 products

The picker performs picking in a single wave instead of executing repetitive delivery-based picks.

Picking Execution

During execution:

  • picker retrieves consolidated quantities,

  • products are staged temporarily,

  • quantities are later sorted/distributed back to respective outbound deliveries,

  • and final loading is executed customer-wise.

This approach minimizes:

  • repeated aisle movement,

  • RF scanning effort,

  • and warehouse congestion.

Distribution Back to Deliveries

After combined picking execution, quantities are redistributed back to the original deliveries.

Material Walmart Costco Albertsons Total Picked

Product 11 EA4 EA7 EA12 EA
Product 22 EA5 EA8 EA15 EA
Product 33 EA6 EA9 EA18 EA

This ensures:

  • customer-level delivery integrity,

  • accurate outbound processing,

  • and optimized warehouse execution simultaneously.

Operational Benefits

Reduced Warehouse Travel Time

Picker visits storage bins once for combined quantities instead of repeating trips per delivery.

Improved Warehouse Productivity

Fewer warehouse tasks and consolidated execution improve throughput.

Lower RF Interaction Effort

Reduced scanning and confirmation steps for warehouse operators.

Better Wave Optimization

Combined picking aligns well with wave management and outbound staging strategies.

Reduced Queue Volume

Warehouse order consolidation minimizes queue and task fragmentation.

Important Design Considerations

Before implementing combined picking, warehouses should evaluate:

  • customer segregation requirements,

  • staging space availability,

  • sorting effort after picking,

  • handling unit strategies,

  • transportation/loading constraints,

  • and warehouse process type alignment.

Combined picking is especially effective for:

  • retail distribution,

  • wholesale fulfillment,

  • FTL outbound scenarios,

  • and high-volume repetitive product movements.

Key Takeaways

This scenario demonstrates how SAP EWM can intelligently optimize outbound execution by consolidating common products across multiple deliveries into a single warehouse execution flow.

Process Summary:

  • 3 deliveries

  • 3 customers

  • Common products across all deliveries

  • 1 combined warehouse order

  • 45 EA picked in a consolidated wave

  • Quantities redistributed back to original deliveries

The result is:

higher warehouse efficiency, reduced picker movement, optimized labor utilization, and improved outbound execution performance.

Conclusion

Combined Picking in SAP EWM is not merely a technical warehouse task consolidation feature — it is a warehouse execution optimization strategy.

When designed correctly, it can significantly improve:

  • warehouse throughput,

  • labor productivity,

  • picking efficiency,

  • and operational scalability in high-volume distribution environments.

As warehouses continue moving toward larger fulfillment volumes and tighter outbound timelines, combined picking becomes an increasingly valuable execution strategy within SAP EWM.

entry.title: SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA のためのSAPノート適用について
entry.content:
お世話になります。

SAPノート3059197 - SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA upgrades に従ってアセスメントを実施するにあたり、
SAPノート 3365856 - Note Analyzer Input File for SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA upgrades. により、ノートアナライザを実行しました。
その結果、
 Preparation SAP Note Check Phase 及び SAP Note Check Phase にて、109件のSAPノートが適用候補としてリストアップされました。
この２つの SAP Note Check PhaseでリストアップされたSAPノートの全ては、SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA upgradesのための修正（先行開発）であり、業務のロジックや業務画面の構成等に影響を与えるものは無いと考えてよいでしょうか？

ひとまず、BASISチームで109件のSAPノートを確認したうえで、業務チームに照会する予定ですが、上記の考え方が正しければ、業務チームへの照会を省略することが出来るのではないかと想定しております。

お手数ですが、ご教示ください。

SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA upgrades の分析結果の全レポートについて、サンプル版がありましたら提供していただけないでしょうか？

現在、5月末の予定で分析結果のレポートを確認する予定でしたが、適用対象のSAPノートの数が多く、業務影響の有無の調査に時間がかかっております。

もし、レポートのサンプル版が入手できれば、SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA upgradesの分析結果としてどのような情報が得られるのかを事前に確認できるので大変助かります。


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: SAP Cloud Alm classification / sub-classification documentation missing
entry.content:
Hello,
The documentaion on the values and usecases for Requirements classification in SAP Cloud ALM is missing. The values are not self-eplanatory, and sometimes controversial.

When use GAP and when WRICEF ?

NOTE TO SAP SUPPORT: I give my approval for SAP Support to reproduce the described issue, while connected to my impacted non-production environments, using any steps provided in the description below or provided later, even if the steps result in a change being made, until the issue is closed.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Alm classification / sub-classification doc...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Please refer to below SAP Community article that provides further explanation regarding the different types in SAP Cloud ALM:

Task and Requirement Management – SAP Cloud ALM or... - SAP Community 

  • Gap: A completely new development which needs to be specified in detail with significant ‘as-is /to-be’ evaluation, often with no technical information or idea how to realize it at the beginning. The technical design is fully done by the developer with the help of a technical design document.  
  • WRICEF (workflows, reports, interfaces, conversions, enhancements, and forms): Typical extensions, where no business background needs to be described. The consultants often know already how to implement and configure. Specifications are often already a mixture of functional and technical design. 
  • Fit (configuration): For changes without coding adjustments, but only customizing. The specification is often an existing standard configuration guide and only customizing documentation needs to be maintained 
  • Non-Functional requirements are used for documentation upload or parameter settings without the need to document a functional specification.  

Best regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Managing Batches in SAP Logistics Management
entry.content:
When working with products that require batch tracking like food items, pharmaceuticals, or materials with expiration dates having a robust batch management system is essential. SAP Logistics Management offers integrated batch handling that connects seamlessly with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, ensuring traceability throughout your warehouse operations.

When working with products that require batch tracking like food items, pharmaceuticals, or materials with expiration dates having a robust batch management system is essential. SAP Logistics Management offers integrated batch handling that connects seamlessly with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, ensuring traceability throughout your warehouse operations.

Batches in SAP Logistics Management

Batches are centrally managed master data that capture critical product information. This data is replicated from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition to SAP Logistics Management using the Schedule Data Replication app in SAP Logistics Management, making it available across all warehouse processes. Pre-requisite for the replication of batches from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition to SAP Logistics Management is, that the batch level is defined on material level in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition customizing.

The following batch characteristics are stored in SAP Logistics Management and can be found in the Display Batches app:

  • Shelf life expiration date
  • Manufacturing date
  • Batch status
  • Country or region of origin
  • Supplier and supplier batch number
  • Whether the batch is marked for deletion

 

How Batches Flow Through Your Warehouse

Inbound Operations

A batch is entered in the inbound delivery item in SAP S/4HANA and the inbound delivery is distributed automatically to SAP Logistics Management. You can view the batch for the item in the Monitor Logistical Demands and Manage Inbound Warehouse Activities apps.

Note: Batch split is not supported.

 

Stock Management

Batches serve as quant-differentiating attributes, meaning your system tracks inventory at the batch level. This granular visibility is available through the Manage Stock app.

 

The Upload Stock app supports now batch-managed products. You can include the batch directly in the template before uploading.

 

Warehouse Tasks

During picking and putaway, batch information is visible in both the Process Warehouse Tasks app and the SAP Warehouse Logistics mobile app (version 1.4), ensuring warehouse operatives always know which batch they're handling.

 

Outbound Operations

A batch is entered in the outbound delivery item in SAP S/4HANA and the outbound delivery is distributed to SAP Logistics Management. The batch follows through picking, packing, staging, and goods issue, maintaining complete traceability until the product leaves your facility. 

Notes:

  • Batch split is not supported
  • The batch is not displayed in transportation relevant documents in SAP Logistics Management

 

Conclusion

Managing batches doesn't have to be complicated. With the right system in place, you gain the visibility and control needed to meet regulatory requirements while keeping operations running smoothly. The above mentioned is just the beginning of the batch journey in SAP Logistics Management so stay tuned.

entry.title: Re: Email Processing & SOST Behavior in S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, email outputs (like payment advices) are not managed through the classic SOST/SCOT transactions as in on-premise systems. Instead, they rely on Output Parameter Determination (OPD), Business Partner email data, and the SAP Output Management framework. If emails are not being sent, the issue is usually due to missing recipient configuration in OPD or restrictions on sender/recipient domains.

Key Steps to Test & Troubleshoot Email Outputs

1. Verify Recipient Setup

Output Parameter Determination (OPD)

Go to the OPD app and check the determination step “Email Recipient” for the relevant document type (e.g., Payment Advice). Ensure a valid recipient email is explicitly defined. This overrides Business Partner data.

Business Partner Master Data

Confirm the email is correctly maintained under the Address tab or Contact Person relationship. Ensure the Valid To date for the contact person is set to 12/31/9999; expired contacts will not be read.

2. Check Allowed Domains
SAP Cloud restricts sender/recipient domains for security. Errors like “Sender domain not allowed” or “Recipient address not allowed” indicate domain whitelisting issues. Work with your Basis/Cloud administrator to ensure your company’s domain is registered in the Communication Arrangement for SMTP.

3. Monitor Email Status
Email Monitoring Apps (e.g., Output Management → Monitor Output) show only limited status. Unlike on-premise SOST, you won’t see full SMTP traces. If the status is “Error,” check the Application Log (SLG1) for SAPconnect-related messages.

4. Background Jobs
Ensure the RSCONN01 job (responsible for processing email queues) is scheduled. Without this, emails remain in “Queued” status.

Having said that, the action plan for you is

  • Re-check OPD rules for Payment Advice → ensure “Email Recipient” is defined.
  • Validate Business Partner email and contact validity.
  • Confirm your sender domain is allowed in Communication Arrangement.
  • Ask Basis team to verify SMTP configuration and background jobs.
  • Use Application Log (SLG1) for deeper error details beyond the monitoring app.

entry.title: Customer-specific Lot Sizing Procedure in ePP/DS
entry.content:
Dear PP/DS Gurus,

I have a questions if I could configure a customer-specific lot sizing procedure and apply it to standard Heuristics, e.g. SAP_PP_002.

Due to manucacturing constraints, my client wants to create planned orders using following lot sizing rules.
- Fixed Lot Size : 30, Max Lot Size : 40

For a new demand, planned orders are basically created based on "Fixed Lot Size". When if uncovered demand quantities remain, the order should be created as follows.
- Case 1 : When uncovered demand quantities > "Max Lot Size" - "Fixed Lot Size", a new planned order should be created by uncovered demand quantities
- Case 2 : When uncovered demand quantities < "Max Lot Size" - "Fixed Lot Size", the uncovered demand quantities should be merged with the last planned order

Here are examaples.
- Case 1 : Demands quantities - 92
                  -> 3 planned orders are created first based on "Fixed Lot Size"
                  -> Planned Order #1(30), Planned Order #2(30), Planned Order #3(30)
                  -> Uncovered demand quantities(2) < "Max Lot Size" - "Fixed Lot Size"(10)
                  -> The final results would be  Planned Order #1(30), Planned Order #2(30), Planned Order #3(32)
- Case 2 : DEmands quantities - 110
                  -> 3 planned orders are created first based on "Fixed Lot Size"
                  -> Planned Order #1(30), Planned Order #2(30), Planned Order #3(30)
                  -> Uncovered demand quantities(20) < "Max Lot Size" - "Fixed Lot Size"(10)
                  -> The final results would be  Planned Order #1(30), Planned Order #2(30), Planned Order #3(30), Planned Order #4(20)

I'm trying to figure out how to configure these requirements using "Lot Sizing Profile", "Rouding Profile - Static, Dynamic, Quantity addition/Subtraction" but I could not find any clues.

So I just want to know if there are any User-Exits or Enhancements to reslove these requiments.

 

Thanks in advance for your cooperation. 

Best Regards,
Sean Lee

entry.title: Re: Output Parameters for Handling Unit
entry.content:
Explore the below options

1) Custom Fields and Logic app
You can add a custom HU field (e.g., “Finalized Indicator”) using Custom Fields and Logic. This field can be exposed in Output Parameter Determination (OPD) as a condition parameter.

2) Released BAdIs (if available)
In Public Cloud, you can only implement SAP‑released BAdIs listed in the Extensibility Catalog. If there is a released BAdI for Handling Unit events (e.g., “HU placed in GI bin”), you can use it to set the custom field. If no such BAdI exists, you cannot inject custom ABAP logic — instead, you must rely on the standard OPD parameters (HU Step Completed, Storage Bin, Storage Process).

3) Fallback if no BAdI exists
Configure OPD rules directly and trigger HU_SHIPPING_LABEL only when HU Step Completed = true and Storage Bin = GI bin. This avoids multiple prints during intermediate HU changes, without needing a custom field.

entry.title: Re: unloading location timing is different
entry.content:
Why the mismatch happens is because of the following:

Delivery Note (DN)
Dates/times are stored in the ERP system (S/4HANA) in local time zone or user time zone.

Freight Order (FO)
When the DN is transferred to TM, the times are converted into the planning time zone defined in TM customizing.

If your ERP system and TM system (or the FO location master data) are set to different time zones, you will see exactly this kind of one‑hour offset.

So the recommended approach is

  • Verify the time zone maintained in the unloading location master data in TM. Align it with the DN’s plant/location time zone.
  • Check the planning time zone in TM customizing. If it differs from the business requirement, adjust it.
  • If you need FO times to always match DN times, ensure both systems (ERP and TM) use the same time zone for the relevant locations.

entry.title: Re: Two Way Match Supplier Criteria
entry.content:
Below are the recommended approaches for transition

Segment suppliers
Apply two‑way only to service vendors, recurring contracts, and low‑risk categories. Keep three‑way for high‑value, physical goods, or new suppliers.

Set tolerance thresholds
Define acceptable variances in PO vs invoice matching to reduce exceptions.

Automate controls
Use AP automation tools to enforce rules consistently and flag anomalies.

Monitor performance
Track disputes, overpayments, and fraud attempts after transition. Adjust supplier eligibility accordingly.

Communicate with suppliers
Ensure they understand the streamlined process and compliance expectations.

entry.content: Check SAP note 3700586
